rebuttals.justification: Synthetic saturation makes sub-claim (a) (deviation helps) unresolvable; affects all GRU arms symmetrically so CONCEDE blocked. Settle via harder variant (card level not in recent history, +0.5-1.0 sigma spike) + real data. Pipeline + seq>>baseline + PLE-adds-nothing purposes survive.
tab_last_raw strawman; seq>>tab measures history access not temporal modeling. Settle via tab_aggregate baseline [last, mean(prior), std(prior)]. GRU-arm comparisons unaffected.

mu/sd + PLE edges fit including fraud last step; deviation is causal. Directionally CONSERVATIVE for seq_dev (raw gets contaminated-normalization advantage). PLE comparison ambiguous. Settle via causal-only refit (prior steps).
8 epochs no convergence check; seq_ple (24-dim) may be undertrained. Ceiling partially mitigates on easy task; matters on harder/real. Settle via loss curves + train-to-plateau.
Parity may be L-specific (running-mean noise scales with length). Settle via length sweep L=3/8/16 on a variant with headroom.
iid split appropriate for stationary synthetic PoC; temporal split already mandated for Step 6 real data.
Mechanism figure on full data is cosmetic (iid -> same shape); no result depends on it.

verdict_rationale: F1-F5 DEFER to named Step-6 tests; no concessions; F6/F7 rebutted. Sub-claim (a) open pending harder control + real data.
